hero.title: 4 Days Phu Quoc.
hero.titleAccent: World's Longest Sea Cable Car, Island Hopping & Aquatopia.
hero.intro: Phu Quoc in four days — the right amount of time for Vietnam's most beautiful island. Day one: Ho Quoc Pagoda (the cliffside temple over the sea), Sanato Beach sunset, and the Night Market. Day two: speedboat across the An Thoi archipelago for four-island hopping — coral snorkelling, May Rut Island beach, Gam Ghi Island shallow coral reef, and the Pineapple Island Aquatopia Water Park. Return on the world's longest sea cable car (7.9 km, 2 hours high above the sea). Day three: free for VinWonders or Vinpearl Safari.
hero.heroImageAlt: Phu Quoc world's longest sea cable car island Vietnam

overview.heading: 4 Days Phu Quoc with World's Longest Sea Cable Car + Aquatopia
body: This four-day package delivers Phu Quoc's two signature experiences in full: the An Thoi island hopping speedboat circuit (coral snorkelling, beach stops, and the Hon Thom Aquatopia Water Park) on day two, and the Hon Thom Cable Car — the world's longest non-stop sea cable car at 7.9 km, crossing from An Thoi mainland to Pineapple Island (Hon Thom) above the open sea — as the return route from the island hopping.

Phu Quoc is Vietnam's largest island, located in the Gulf of Thailand 50 km off the Cambodian coast. It receives more sun than mainland Vietnam and has a distinct dry season (November–May) when the sea is clear and calm. The An Thoi archipelago at the southern tip of Phu Quoc comprises 15 small islands with coral reefs at 1–3 metres depth — the most accessible coral snorkelling in Vietnam without requiring scuba training.

Ho Quoc Pagoda on day one is a signature Phu Quoc sight that most island-focused packages miss: a Buddhist complex built into a granite cliff above the sea on the island's east coast, reached by a staircase through the rock with sea panoramas from each landing. The temple's position — jutting out over the water on a ridge — gives the most dramatic religious architecture setting in the Gulf of Thailand.

highlights.heading: Why This 4-Day Phu Quoc Package Stands Apart

items.title: World's Longest Sea Cable Car — 7.9 km
items.body: The Hon Thom Cable Car holds the Guinness World Record as the longest non-stop sea cable car in the world: 7.9 kilometres from the An Thoi mainland terminal to Hon Thom (Pineapple Island). The gondola ascends to 175 metres above the sea surface, crossing three small islands and the open water of the An Thoi Strait. The journey takes approximately 15 minutes. On clear days the Cambodian coast is visible to the northwest. The cable car return (from the island to the mainland) is included in the island hopping package.
items.imageAlt: Phu Quoc world's longest sea cable car Hon Thom island Vietnam

items.title: Aquatopia Water Park — Full Day Included
items.body: Aquatopia is a large water park on Hon Thom (Pineapple Island), accessible only via the cable car. Included with the cable car ticket: all water slides, wave pools, lazy river, and pool facilities. The park has slides for all ages, from family-friendly gentle runs to the steep drops for thrill-seekers. The buffet lunch at Mango Restaurant (on the island, 100+ dish Vietnamese and international buffet) is included. The Eagle Eye Observatory on the island's highest point offers a 360-degree view of the An Thoi archipelago from 100 metres elevation.
items.imageAlt: Aquatopia Water Park Phu Quoc Hon Thom island Vietnam

items.title: Four-Island Snorkelling Circuit
items.body: The speedboat island hopping circuit visits four islands in the An Thoi archipelago: Coral Park (structured coral reef, snorkelling at 1–5m depth, sea walking optional at own expense approximately RM80), May Rut Island (white sand beach for swimming and rest, 45 minutes), Gam Ghi Island (shallow coral reef at 1–3m depth, excellent for non-swimmers to see coral from the surface), and Hon Thom (Pineapple Island) for the Aquatopia and Eagle Eye Observatory. The full circuit takes a full day — departure from An Thoi Harbour at 07:00–08:30.
items.imageAlt: Phu Quoc island hopping speedboat coral snorkelling Vietnam

items.title: Ho Quoc Pagoda — Cliffside Buddhist Temple
items.body: Ho Quoc Pagoda (Truc Lam Ho Quoc) is built into a granite cliff on Phu Quoc's eastern coast, with the temple complex spanning several levels connected by staircases cut through the rock. Founded in 2012, the pagoda is named for the island (Ho Quoc means 'Moat of the Nation') and features a white stupa on the cliff's highest point. The view from the stupa is across the Gulf of Thailand with no land visible to the horizon — one of the most dramatic temple settings in Vietnam. The approach is through tropical forest on the east coast road, far from the resort development on the west.
items.imageAlt: Ho Quoc Pagoda cliffside Buddhist temple Phu Quoc Vietnam

days.title: Arrive Phu Quoc — Pagoda & Night Market
days.description: Arrive at Phu Quoc International Airport. Transfer to hotel (approximately 20–30 minutes). Afternoon: Ho Quoc Pagoda — Buddhist complex built into granite cliff above the east coast sea, with staircase through the rock and sea panoramas from each level. Sanato Beach (Sunset Town area) for late afternoon swimming and the sunset view over the bay. Duong Dong Night Market — Phu Quoc's busiest evening market, with fresh grilled seafood (pick your own fish, prawn, or crab to be grilled), pepper crab (Phu Quoc black pepper is the finest in Vietnam), and the island's famous fish sauce. Dinner at Night Market.
suggestedActivities: Fresh grilled seafood and Phu Quoc pepper crab

days.title: 4-Island Hopping + Cable Car + Aquatopia
days.description: 07:00–08:30: Shuttle to An Thoi Harbour for 4-island speedboat tour. Coral Park: snorkelling equipment provided, coral reef at 1–5m depth. Optional sea walking at own expense (approximately RM80). May Rut Island: white sand beach stop for swimming (45 minutes). Gam Ghi Island: shallow 1–3m coral reef, good for non-snorkellers to view coral from the surface. Pineapple Island (Hon Thom): full day at Aquatopia Water Park (all rides and facilities included), buffet lunch at Mango Restaurant (100+ dishes, all included), Eagle Eye Observatory (360-degree views). 16:00: Return via world's longest sea cable car (7.9 km, 15 minutes, 175m above sea). Transfer back to hotel.
suggestedActivities: May Rut Island white sand beach
Buffet lunch at Mango Restaurant (100+ dishes)
16:00 return via world's longest sea cable car (7.9km)

days.title: Free Day — Optional VinWonders or Safari
days.description: Full free day in Phu Quoc. Optional VinWonders Phu Quoc (RM245 per person, own expense) — a large entertainment park with rides, 5D cinema, themed zones, and a swimming lagoon. Optional Vinpearl Safari Phu Quoc (RM245 per person, own expense) — one of the largest open wildlife parks in Southeast Asia, with free-roaming African and Asian animals (giraffes, zebras, lions, bears). Combination VinWonders + Safari (RM405, own expense). Optional evening: Sunset Town pier area (Kiss Bridge, ferris wheel, floating market, gondola). Dinner at own arrangement.
suggestedActivities: Optional Sunset Town evening (Kiss Bridge, ferris wheel)

days.title: Phu Quoc Free Morning — Depart
days.description: Free morning on the island. Optional: Long Beach (Bai Truong) for a final swim or walk along the longest beach on Phu Quoc's west coast. Sao Beach visit (Bai Sao — rated one of the most beautiful beaches in Asia, with white powder sand and turquoise water, 30 minutes from Duong Dong town). Phu Quoc fish sauce factory (nuoc mam — Phu Quoc black-barrel fermented anchovy sauce, the finest in Vietnam). Transfer to Phu Quoc International Airport per agreed schedule.
suggestedActivities: Optional: Sao Beach (finest white sand on the island)
Optional: Phu Quoc fish sauce factory

whyBook.heading: Phu Quoc Made Easy — By Malaysia's Trusted Agency Since 1988
whyBook.body: Phu Quoc's best experiences require coordination: the island hopping speedboat timing, the cable car capacity during peak season, and the Aquatopia park crowd timing all matter. Dhesu has been arranging Phu Quoc packages for Malaysian travellers since the island opened to international tourism. We pre-book the speedboat, the cable car, and the park pass — nothing is left to queue at the ticket window.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. Phu Quoc packages are among our most booked Vietnam island itineraries for Malaysian travellers.

points.title: 35+ Years of Vietnam Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to Vietnam. Our Phu Quoc island hopping and cable car arrangements are pre-confirmed and reliable.

points.title: Cable Car + 4-Island Circuit Fully Included
points.body: The world's longest sea cable car (7.9km return), all four island stops, Aquatopia full-day pass, Eagle Eye Observatory, and buffet lunch are all included. No separate ticketing on the island.

points.title: Pay for 3, 4th Goes Free
points.body: Groups of 4 travelling together pay for only 3 — the 4th person travels completely free. Confirm this promotion at time of booking.

sections.heading: 4 Day Phu Quoc Tour with World's Longest Sea Cable Car from Malaysia
sections.body:
The 4-day Phu Quoc package with the world's longest sea cable car is the most complete Phu Quoc itinerary for Malaysian travellers. Phu Quoc is directly connected to Kuala Lumpur (AirAsia KUL-PQC direct, approximately 1.5 hours), making it one of the closest international island destinations for Malaysians.

The Hon Thom Cable Car (7.9 km) holds the Guinness World Record as the longest non-stop sea cable car in the world. Combined with the 4-island snorkelling and speedboat circuit, the Aquatopia Water Park, and Ho Quoc Pagoda — this package covers all of Phu Quoc's essential attractions in four days. The 'Pay for 3, 4th free' promotion makes group travel particularly good value.

faq.heading: Questions About the 4-Day Phu Quoc Cable Car Package

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Vietnam?
items.answer: No. Malaysian passport holders enter Vietnam visa-free for up to 30 days. Phu Quoc International Airport has direct flights from Kuala Lumpur (AirAsia). No advance visa required.

items.question: Is the island hopping suitable for non-swimmers?
items.answer: Yes. The speedboat and all island stops are accessible regardless of swimming ability. At the Coral Park, non-swimmers can observe the reef from the boat surface. At May Rut Island and Gam Ghi Island, the water is very shallow (1–3m at Gam Ghi) so snorkelling gear is usable in standing depth. Sea walking (a helmet dive system) at Coral Park is optional and does not require swimming ability.

items.question: When is the best time to visit Phu Quoc?
items.answer: November to March is peak season — dry weather, calm sea, excellent visibility for snorkelling. April and May remain mostly dry. The rainy season (June–October) can have rough seas that affect island hopping schedules, though this is not guaranteed. The island hopping can be cancelled if sea conditions are unsafe — the operator will reschedule to the next available day.

items.question: What are VinWonders and Vinpearl Safari?
items.answer: Both are operated by Vinpearl on the northern tip of Phu Quoc. VinWonders is a large theme park with rides, 5D cinema, themed zones, and a beachfront swimming lagoon. Vinpearl Safari is an open wildlife park with African and Asian animals in free-roaming enclosures (giraffes, lions, bears, zebras, orangutans). Both are a 30–40 minute drive from central Phu Quoc town. Entry is RM245 each or RM405 for the combination ticket. Neither is included in the package — they are Day 3 optional activities at own expense.
